name: Shoko Relay
overview: A custom Plex metadata provider and automation toolset for integrating Plex and AnimeThemes with Shoko Server.

releases.release_notes:
**Changes:**
- Switch from Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Versioning.ApiExplorer v5.1.0 to Asp.Versioning.Mvc.ApiExplorer v10.0.0 to account for Shoko Server changes
- Add Asp.Versioning using directives to all controllers to resolve [ApiVersion] attribute namespace changes
- Update PurgeUnusedReleases call in ShokoImportService to reflect new signature in Shoko.Abstractions
- Properly dispose CancellationTokenSource instances in VfsWatcher
- Optimize dynamic CSS generation in DashboardController by replacing StringBuilder with raw string literals
- Restrict automation interval, UTC offset, and series ID filter inputs to  acceptable values on the dashboard
- Replace duplicate animethemes section icons with more appropriates ones and add a small animethemes logo badge to them
- Bump Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.61

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.8...v0.14.0

**Changes:**
- Add getWebmSeasonParams to animethemes.js to dynamically calculate the current anime season string based on the client date
- Update dashboard.cshtml to include the new "Download Current Season" button which points to the WebM download endpoint
- Add TaskAtWebmDownload to the dashboard tasks dictionary for loading spinner state tracking
- Add Audit method to VfsBuilder to scan and remove orphaned series folders, broken symlinks, and empty subdirectories
- Add a new "Audit VFS" button to the dashboard using the database-check-outline icon
- Update LogHelper to generate a detailed shoko-vfs-audit-report.log listing removed items
- Add an optional forceRemote boolean parameter to ImageHelper.GetImageUrl defaulting to false to preserve local Shoko API routing by default
- Update ImageHelper.GenerateImageArray to dynamically pass forceRemote based on the AddEveryImage configuration state
- Hardcode forceRemote to true for TMDB season posters in PlexMetadata to support the temporary addEveryImage override workaround
- Resolve Plex transcode failures for alternate images caused by Plex Cloud Proxy attempting to access local private IPs
- Change several dashboard icons to be more "database" themed

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.7...v0.13.8

**Changes:**
- Update loadVfsTree in browser.js to only append the virtual "All" tab if there are multiple physical roots detected
- Upgrade the Theme.mp3 cache to store pipe-delimited metadata containing path, slug, and upgrade status
- Add AuditAsync to AnimeThemesMp3Generator to check for available openings on non-OP themes and repair missing local slugs via ID3 tags
- Integrate the MP3 Audit action button and task polling on the dashboard and document the new endpoints in README/Controller.md
- Fix a VFS Browser regression where the "Refresh VFS Folder" button was not working by restricting the representative file search inside browser.js to only check standard Season and Specials folders
- Replace fragile index-based default checks in FetchThemeAsync with a prioritization search to make default Theme.mp3 selection more robust
- Extract primary series (from a Shoko group) descriptions using language and sanitization settings and apply them to Plex collections
- Add oninput event listeners to at-slug and at-offset on the dashboard to filter out invalid characters
- Add GetCurrentSeasonRange to AnimeThemesHelper to centralize date calculations with a one-month early buffer
- Replace the explicit string-based Season filter with a boolean Seasonal flag for MP3 generation
- Update WebM download validation to default to the current year if a season is provided without a year
- Bump Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.58

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.6...v0.13.7

**Changes:**
- Remove numerical prefixes from plugin page names in ShokoRelay.cs since Shoko Server no longer sorts them alphabetically
- Include manual folder exclusions in the readme like previous versions did as a recommended safety net
- Add a list of downloaded filenames to WebmDownloadResult and log them in at-webm-download-report.log
- Add a list of mapped file paths to SourceLinkResult and log them in shoko-map-symlinks-report.log
- Track the specific images (thumbs, posters, backdrops, logos) uploaded to Shoko in ImageSyncResult and list them in plex-images-sync-report.log
- Swap out the general query parameter 'q' for the explicit 'filter[anime][name]' parameter in FetchAnimePageAsync
- Update BuildAtMp3Report in LogHelper to append error and skip reason messages to the generated log
- Add GetLogsList endpoint to DashboardController to serialize file listings
- Add Task Logs section to the bottom of the dashboard for viewing the list of logs
- Document the logs section and listing endpoints in the Readme/Controller.md
- Add `forceScan: true` to ScheduleScanForManagedFolders inside ShokoImportService to bypass the per file scan limit
- Bump Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.55

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.5...v0.13.6

**Changes:**
- Add an optional Destination property to AnimeThemesWebmQuery
- Update AnimeThemesWebmDownloader to respect the destination override if it is a valid managed folder or absolute path
- Update managed folder filtering to only skip folders strictly configured as source without destination
- Implement lock-free fast paths inside ConfigProvider.GetSettings and VfsShared.GetIgnoredFolderNames
- Change the filter box from using absolute to flex positioning across the browser/player
- In the player's landscape layout move the filter box from the bottom of the tree to the top below the title
- Rename "Theme.mp3 Path Mappings" to "Path Mappings" in RelayConfig and settings help modal
- Add a global max-width media query to tree.css to stack the header elements on narrow screens
- Add a max-width media query to browser.css to ensure the tabs stay in the first header row
- General layout tweaks for better header/dashboard rendering below 800px width
- Bump Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.53

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.4...v0.13.5

**Changes:**
- Migrate VFS Blueprint reading in ShokoController from Newtonsoft.Json to System.Text.Json to drastically reduce memory allocation for large libraries
- Implement atomic File.Move writes in VfsBuilder to prevent file corruption and JSON truncation during concurrent operations
- Wrap the VFS Tree endpoint logic in a try/catch to safely return empty arrays instead of throwing 500 errors on invalid cache files
- Serialize the VFS Browser tree response directly with System.Text.Json using a ContentResult avoiding the backend Newtonsoft implementation completely
- Revert inclusion of pre-release abstraction tags in the manifest (the backend isn't configured to parse said tags)
- Introduce a secondary filename-based lookup dictionary for AnimeThemes mappings
- Reuse curated metadata and remap file paths if the user's folder structure does not match the curated map perfectly 
- Bump Shoko Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.47
- Various performance optimizations/tweaks
 
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.3...v0.13.4

**Changes:**
- Moved Video Player and VFS Browser to top-level routes (/player and /browser)
- Split GetControllerPage into GetDashboardPage, GetPlayerPage, and GetBrowserPage actions
- Moved static asset delivery to a dedicated GetAssetFile catch-all action under the dashboard path
- Updated the pathname split regular expression in script.js to support the new player and browser routes
- Introduced a ServePage helper method that dynamically constructs safe base tags
- Updated Dashboard UI links and readme/controller.md documentation to match the new routes
- Changed the "Video Player" page name to "AnimeThemes Player"
- Remove the edited event type from the build-release trigger to prevent workflow spam
- Bumped Shoko.Abstractions to 6.0.0-alpha.46

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.2...v0.13.3

**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the player with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended
- The WebUI installation method is now live

**Changes:**
- Allow batch Theme MP3 generation for deeply nested directories and track if a theme has already been generated for a given AniDB ID
- Added validation checks to single and batch MP3 generation pipelines to detect and reject VFS root folder segments
- Implemented an Audio Only toggle button and `V` hotkey to hide the video layout and stop active rendering (player page)
- Implemented the `R` hotkey to restart the currently active theme from the beginning (player page)
- Parse mapped AnimeThemes that do not correspond to an anime in the local Shoko collection and add them to the video player tree
- Group unmapped anime using physical base name extraction and append them to a unified "Missing from Collection" branch
- Add sorting logic to `player.js` to force the "Missing from Collection" branch to the top of the browser list
- Added `AnimeThemesWebmDownloader` and exposed it as an HTTP POST endpoint for fetching and organizing .webm files into Year/Season directories
- Fixed the "jump to" action sometime failing on the player page
- Enhanced the build workflow to inject release notes into the manifest
- Updated the readme/controller.md to account for the new changes
- Bumped Shoko Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.44

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.1...v0.13.2

**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the vfs browser with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended

**Changes:**
- Map active AnimeThemes files directly into the VFS Browser's blueprint database during generation allowing them to be seen
- Natural sort the season folders in the vfs browser and place them after non season folders in the tree
- Prune redundant, multi-line record instantiation blocks inside AnimeThemesMapping and its helper LoadThemeMappings
- Update browser.js index mapping to support metadata link resolving for all extra season folders
- Dynamically append the resolved Plex URL to the Plex Web App button's tooltip on hover
- Implement prefix-based "m{ShokoID}" and "a{AniDBID}" search filtering to target series IDs directly
- Restrict numeric AniDB ID filtering in the VFS Browser to only match when prefixed with "a"
- Force single column dashboard layout at 925px media width and set header logo and title margins to 0
- This ensures that the header text doesn't overflow at 700px window width (for the WebUI iframe)
- Parse CSharpier output in the lint workflow to emit native GitHub inline error annotations
- Generate a rich Markdown report and append it directly to the GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
- Utilize dotnet tool restore to download CSharpier based on the committed .config/dotnet-tools.json manifest
- Refactor MapHelper/VfsBuilder/VfsWatcher with some general performance/style/stability tweaks
- Remove redundant backing fields across the project
- Directly reference modern primary constructor parameters across all refactored service methods
- Evaluate input.value on initial search interaction setup to determine the clear button's visibility on the player/browser pages
- Bump Shoko Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.38

**Pull Requests:**
* feat: add plugin page by @revam in https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/pull/8

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.13.0...v0.13.1

This is one of the larger releases in a while feature wise but now I can say that all of the extended additions that were planned are complete.

**Notes:**
- Force refreshing the dashboard/browser/player with `Ctrl-F5` is recommended
- The "Collection Posters Root Path" setting no longer exists and was replaced with the "Collection Images Root Path" (there is no migration)

**Changes:**
- Upgraded VFS series pruning logic to resolve directories directly via active managed folders, ensuring empty or deleted series folders and their metadata are recursively cleaned up.
- Simplified Shoko VFS import ignore rules by evaluating the full absolute path of scanned files.
- Added a dynamic Plex Web App link to the authentication panel, automatically resolving the best connection URI from the discovered servers.
- Optimized path mapping translation speed and memory allocations in the Plex client by introducing thread-safe caching and eliminating redundant LINQ allocations.
- Optimized smart collection artwork scans and Shoko import scans to eliminate database-heavy loops by resolving global roots once and utilizing thread-safe caching for ignored folder names.
- Expanded collection generation to support mapping, syncing, and automatic local disk pruning for collection posters, backdrops, clear logos, and square art.
- Refactored watched-state syncing to Plex to support multiple Plex RatingKeys per Shoko ID, ensuring all matching virtual segments of split or grouped episodes are correctly scrobbled.
- Implemented bi-directional watched state syncing and webhook playback progress tracking (Plex to Shoko) to populate the "Continue Watching" section in Shoko's WebUI.
- Introduced an "Include Progress" toggle to the configuration settings and sync menu modal to control progress synchronization, complete with automatic checks to prevent progress updates from overwriting fully-watched states.
- Cleaned up the codebase by migrating inline namespace declarations to modern file-scoped usings, and removing several unused, unreferenced, or redundant helper methods, constants, and config parameters.
- Polished the dashboard, browser, and player layouts with CSS flexbox styling, robust tooltip positioning for disabled states, optimized web font subsets, and a visual progress bar for timed toast notifications.
- Bumped Shoko.Abstractions to v6.0.0-alpha.32.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/natyusha/ShokoRelay/compare/v0.12.7...v0.13.0
